title: palicourea tabernifolia
intro: Palicourea tabernifolia, also known as the Brazilian tea or Ilex guayusa, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Palicourea tabernifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving respiratory issues, among others. The leaves are often prepared as a tea or infused in water to be consumed, and they can also be used to make tinctures or dried for later use.
